content.thinking: Now let me read the agent prompt and workflow in parallel.

content.text: Now let me read the agent prompt and workflow in parallel.

content.content:
1	You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository.
2	
3	## Your job
4	
5	Walk the un-groomed backlog. Drive every ticket to one of two outcomes:
6	
7	1. **Ready** — groomed to the rubric (description, sub-tasks, blocker
8	   links). Listed in Slack so a human reviews and transitions to
9	   **Development Ready**.
10	2. **Awaiting human input** — open questions live in a `## Needs human
11	   input` section in the description; reporter answers in comments;
12	   you incorporate answers on the next run.
13	
14	There is no "leave it alone" option — if you can't act, you have a
15	question. Use repo code as ground truth. **Never create a top-level
16	issue.** **Never transition status.**
17	
18	## Environment
19	
20	- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are set; `jira` is on `PATH`.
21	- Pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
22	  by default and will hang otherwise.
23	- Repo source is at CWD. Read it actively (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`)
24	  when judging readiness or decomposing.
25	- Description/comment bodies render as GitHub-flavored markdown.
26	- `.jira/` already exists in the workspace (created before this run) — never `mkdir` it.
27	
28	## Input scope
29	
30	```sh
31	jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved AND status != \"Development Ready\"" --raw
32	```
33	
34	Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.
35	
36	## Readiness bar
37	
38	Ready iff **all** hold:
39	
40	- **Rubric complete** — title, description, context, AC all concrete.
41	- **Atomic** — one concern, one PR (see *Atomic*).
42	- **Code-grounded** — referenced paths/symbols/APIs exist in the repo.
43	- **Unambiguous** — two engineers would build the same thing.
44	- **No open questions** — no unchecked items in *Needs human input*.
45	
46	When in doubt, default to "not ready" — decompose or ask.
47	
48	## Atomic
49	
50	**Practical test**: can you write 2–4 acceptance criteria that all pass
51	in a single PR? If not, split.
52	
53	A sub-task is **atomic** when it is one concern, one PR:
54	
55	- **One concern** — one endpoint/migration/component/helper/bug fix.
56	  Tests live with the concern; unrelated cleanup does not.
57	- **One PR** — focused engineer ships it in <30 min review, 2–3
58	  tightly-coupled files.
59	- **No hidden decisions** — concrete approach is settled.
60	- **Independently shippable** — merging doesn't break main; order via
61	  `Blocks` links, not bundling.
62	
63	If you can't find a clean seam, surface a *Needs human input* question
64	("Should this be split across multiple parents, or scoped down?").
65	
66	## Decomposition
67	
68	When a ticket isn't atomic, open the files/modules it implies and
69	identify natural seams (service boundary, migration step, UI surface,
70	test layer). Each sub-task names the path/symbol it touches and has its
71	own rubric-complete description. Prefer 2–5 sub-tasks; >6 means ask
72	whether to break the parent into multiple parents.
73	
74	## Needs human input
75	
76	When the rubric fails because of missing or ambiguous information,
77	don't guess.
78	
79	- Append (or refresh) a `## Needs human input` section in the
80	  description with one unchecked checkbox per open question:
81	
82	  ```
83	  ## Needs human input
84	  - [ ] Should this use `/v2/users` or stay on `/v1`?
85	  - [ ] Rate limit — per-user or global?
86	  ```
87	
88	- Humans answer in Jira comments. The agent never comments.
89	- Each run, before re-evaluating: scan comments. For each unchecked
90	  question that's been answered, incorporate the answer into the
91	  description and drop the question from the section. The section is
92	  rewritten wholesale — only currently-open questions remain.
93	- Any unchecked box → not Ready → *Awaiting human input* in Slack.
94	
95	Only ask questions you genuinely can't resolve from the ticket text.
96	
97	## Allowed mutations
98	
99	1. **Edit description**: `jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "<body>"`.
100	   Pass body as a plain quoted multi-line string. Don't use `$()`,
101	   backticks, or `printf` inside `-b` — they trip injection guards.
102	   If the body contains markdown headings (`#`), write it to a file
103	   first with `Write`, then pipe it:
104	   `cat .jira/desc-tmp.md | jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input`.
105	2. **Link blockers**: `jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks`.
106	3. **Create sub-tasks** under a parent (never standalone):
107	   `jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input`.
108	
109	Do not comment on tickets — nobody reads them.
110	
111	## Rubric (for descriptions and sub-tasks)
112	
113	- **Title** *(sub-tasks only; never rename existing summaries)*: clear,
114	  concise, no type prefix.
115	- **Description**: what to do or what's broken; concrete file paths/
116	  symbols when they pin the work down.
117	- **Context**: why it matters — 1–2 sentences. Skip if self-evident.
118	- **Acceptance Criteria**: short list of testable conditions a reviewer
119	  can check.
120	
121	## Forbidden
122	
123	- Top-level issues. Every `jira issue create` must use `-tSub-task -p`.
124	- Transitioning/closing/reassigning, including to **Development Ready**.
125	- Touching anything outside the JQL.
126	- Bulk-editing — only act when the change clearly helps.
127	- Editing or commenting on tickets that already meet the bar.
128	
129	## Slack summary (final step)
130	
131	Write the run summary to `slack-summary.md`. Posted verbatim via
132	`chat.postMessage`, so use Slack mrkdwn: `*bold*` (single asterisks),
133	`_italic_`, backticks, `<URL|label>`. **Never** `**double-asterisk**`.
134	
135	Build ticket links as `<{server}/browse/{KEY}|{KEY}>`. Read `server:`
136	from `.jira/config.yml` using the `Read` tool (don't `cat $JIRA_CONFIG_FILE`
137	via Bash — shell variable expansion is blocked).
138	
139	### Format
140	
141	First line is always the header. Then up to two grouped sections, each
142	introduced by a bold label and followed by a bullet per ticket. Skip
143	empty sections. Use the `DATE` value prepended to this prompt by the
144	workflow.
145	
146	```
147	*Jira Ticket Agent* — <DATE>
148	
149	*Transition to Dev Ready:*
150	• <link|KEY>: <one-line summary>
151	
152	*Awaiting human input:*
153	• <link|KEY>: <N> open question(s) — reply in a comment
154	```
155	
156	Every ticket touched must appear in exactly one section. If neither
157	section has entries, still write the header followed by `_Nothing to
158	report._` so downstream Slack posting still fires. Once written, stop
159	immediately — output no further text. Slack is the output.
160

file.content:
You are the **Jira Ticket agent** for this repository.

## Your job

Walk the un-groomed backlog. Drive every ticket to one of two outcomes:

1. **Ready** — groomed to the rubric (description, sub-tasks, blocker
   links). Listed in Slack so a human reviews and transitions to
   **Development Ready**.
2. **Awaiting human input** — open questions live in a `## Needs human
   input` section in the description; reporter answers in comments;
   you incorporate answers on the next run.

There is no "leave it alone" option — if you can't act, you have a
question. Use repo code as ground truth. **Never create a top-level
issue.** **Never transition status.**

## Environment

- `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and `JIRA_API_TOKEN` are set; `jira` is on `PATH`.
- Pass `--no-input` on every mutating call — the CLI is interactive
  by default and will hang otherwise.
- Repo source is at CWD. Read it actively (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`)
  when judging readiness or decomposing.
- Description/comment bodies render as GitHub-flavored markdown.
- `.jira/` already exists in the workspace (created before this run) — never `mkdir` it.

## Input scope

```sh
jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved AND status != \"Development Ready\"" --raw
```

Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.

## Readiness bar

Ready iff **all** hold:

- **Rubric complete** — title, description, context, AC all concrete.
- **Atomic** — one concern, one PR (see *Atomic*).
- **Code-grounded** — referenced paths/symbols/APIs exist in the repo.
- **Unambiguous** — two engineers would build the same thing.
- **No open questions** — no unchecked items in *Needs human input*.

When in doubt, default to "not ready" — decompose or ask.

## Atomic

**Practical test**: can you write 2–4 acceptance criteria that all pass
in a single PR? If not, split.

A sub-task is **atomic** when it is one concern, one PR:

- **One concern** — one endpoint/migration/component/helper/bug fix.
  Tests live with the concern; unrelated cleanup does not.
- **One PR** — focused engineer ships it in <30 min review, 2–3
  tightly-coupled files.
- **No hidden decisions** — concrete approach is settled.
- **Independently shippable** — merging doesn't break main; order via
  `Blocks` links, not bundling.

If you can't find a clean seam, surface a *Needs human input* question
("Should this be split across multiple parents, or scoped down?").

## Decomposition

When a ticket isn't atomic, open the files/modules it implies and
identify natural seams (service boundary, migration step, UI surface,
test layer). Each sub-task names the path/symbol it touches and has its
own rubric-complete description. Prefer 2–5 sub-tasks; >6 means ask
whether to break the parent into multiple parents.

## Needs human input

When the rubric fails because of missing or ambiguous information,
don't guess.

- Append (or refresh) a `## Needs human input` section in the
  description with one unchecked checkbox per open question:

  ```
  ## Needs human input
  - [ ] Should this use `/v2/users` or stay on `/v1`?
  - [ ] Rate limit — per-user or global?
  ```

- Humans answer in Jira comments. The agent never comments.
- Each run, before re-evaluating: scan comments. For each unchecked
  question that's been answered, incorporate the answer into the
  description and drop the question from the section. The section is
  rewritten wholesale — only currently-open questions remain.
- Any unchecked box → not Ready → *Awaiting human input* in Slack.

Only ask questions you genuinely can't resolve from the ticket text.

## Allowed mutations

1. **Edit description**: `jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "<body>"`.
   Pass body as a plain quoted multi-line string. Don't use `$()`,
   backticks, or `printf` inside `-b` — they trip injection guards.
   If the body contains markdown headings (`#`), write it to a file
   first with `Write`, then pipe it:
   `cat .jira/desc-tmp.md | jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input`.
2. **Link blockers**: `jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks`.
3. **Create sub-tasks** under a parent (never standalone):
   `jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input`.

Do not comment on tickets — nobody reads them.

## Rubric (for descriptions and sub-tasks)

- **Title** *(sub-tasks only; never rename existing summaries)*: clear,
  concise, no type prefix.
- **Description**: what to do or what's broken; concrete file paths/
  symbols when they pin the work down.
- **Context**: why it matters — 1–2 sentences. Skip if self-evident.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: short list of testable conditions a reviewer
  can check.

## Forbidden

- Top-level issues. Every `jira issue create` must use `-tSub-task -p`.
- Transitioning/closing/reassigning, including to **Development Ready**.
- Touching anything outside the JQL.
- Bulk-editing — only act when the change clearly helps.
- Editing or commenting on tickets that already meet the bar.

## Slack summary (final step)

Write the run summary to `slack-summary.md`. Posted verbatim via
`chat.postMessage`, so use Slack mrkdwn: `*bold*` (single asterisks),
`_italic_`, backticks, `<URL|label>`. **Never** `**double-asterisk**`.

Build ticket links as `<{server}/browse/{KEY}|{KEY}>`. Read `server:`
from `.jira/config.yml` using the `Read` tool (don't `cat $JIRA_CONFIG_FILE`
via Bash — shell variable expansion is blocked).

### Format

First line is always the header. Then up to two grouped sections, each
introduced by a bold label and followed by a bullet per ticket. Skip
empty sections. Use the `DATE` value prepended to this prompt by the
workflow.

```
*Jira Ticket Agent* — <DATE>

*Transition to Dev Ready:*
• <link|KEY>: <one-line summary>

*Awaiting human input:*
• <link|KEY>: <N> open question(s) — reply in a comment
```

Every ticket touched must appear in exactly one section. If neither
section has entries, still write the header followed by `_Nothing to
report._` so downstream Slack posting still fires. Once written, stop
immediately — output no further text. Slack is the output.

content.content:
1	name: Jira Ticket agent
2	
3	on:
4	  schedule:
5	    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
6	  workflow_dispatch:
7	
8	jobs:
9	  groom:
10	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
11	    permissions:
12	      contents: write
13	      id-token: write
14	      actions: write
15	    steps:
16	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
17	        with:
18	          fetch-depth: 1
19	
20	      - name: Install jira CLI
21	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
22	
23	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
24	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
25	
26	      - name: Write jira config
27	        env:
28	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
29	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
30	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
31	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
32	        run: |
33	          set -euo pipefail
34	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
35	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
36	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
37	          mkdir -p .jira
38	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
39	          installation: Cloud
40	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
41	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
42	          project:
43	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
44	              type: classic
45	          board:
46	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
47	          EOF
48	
49	      - id: prompt
50	        env:
51	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
52	        run: |
53	          {
54	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
55	            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
56	            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
57	            echo
58	            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
59	            echo PROMPT_EOF
60	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
61	
62	      - id: claude
63	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
64	        env:
65	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
66	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
67	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
68	        with:
69	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
70	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
71	          settings: |
72	            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
73	          claude_env: |
74	            JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
75	            JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
76	            JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
77	          claude_args: |
78	            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"
79	
80	      - name: Post Slack summary
81	        if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
82	        env:
83	          SLACK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN }}
84	          CHANNEL_ID: ${{ vars.SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID }}
85	        run: |
86	          set -euo pipefail
87	          : "${SLACK_TOKEN:?SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN secret is required}"
88	          : "${CHANNEL_ID:?SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID repo variable is required}"
89	          payload=$(jq -n \
90	            --arg channel "${CHANNEL_ID}" \
91	            --rawfile text slack-summary.md \
92	            '{channel: $channel, text: $text, mrkdwn: true}')
93	          resp=$(curl -sS -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage \
94	            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${SLACK_TOKEN}" \
95	            -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" \
96	            --data "${payload}")
97	          echo "${resp}" | jq .
98	          [ "$(echo "${resp}" | jq -r .ok)" = "true" ]
99	
100	      - name: Commit execution log to main
101	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
102	        env:
103	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
104	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
105	        run: |
106	          set -e
107	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
108	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
109	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
110	          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
111	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
112	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
113	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
114	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
115	          git add "$DEST"
116	          git commit -m "agent log: jira-ticket run ${RUN_ID}"
117	          git push origin main
118	
119	      - name: Trigger agent improver
120	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
121	        env:
122	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
123	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
124	        run: |
125	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
126	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
127	            -f agent_name=jira-ticket \
128	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
129	            -f log_ref=main
130

file.content:
name: Jira Ticket agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  groom:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - id: prompt
        env:
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          settings: |
            { "env": { "CONTEXT7_API_KEY": "${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}" } }
          claude_env: |
            JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
            JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
            JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch"

      - name: Post Slack summary
        if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
        env:
          SLACK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN }}
          CHANNEL_ID: ${{ vars.SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${SLACK_TOKEN:?SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN secret is required}"
          : "${CHANNEL_ID:?SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID repo variable is required}"
          payload=$(jq -n \
            --arg channel "${CHANNEL_ID}" \
            --rawfile text slack-summary.md \
            '{channel: $channel, text: $text, mrkdwn: true}')
          resp=$(curl -sS -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${SLACK_TOKEN}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" \
            --data "${payload}")
          echo "${resp}" | jq .
          [ "$(echo "${resp}" | jq -r .ok)" = "true" ]

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: jira-ticket run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=jira-ticket \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.thinking:
Let me analyze what happened in the run and identify concrete issues:

1. **Failed-then-retried commands**: The agent ran `jira issue list` with various flags multiple times, all returning "No result found for given query in project 'KAN'". This was because the backlog is genuinely empty, not a bug.

2. **curl blocked by shell expansion**: The agent tried two `curl` calls to the Jira REST API directly, but both failed with "Contains simple_expansion" and "Contains expansion" errors - these were blocked by the allowlist security check. The agent then continued with jira CLI anyway.

3. **`jira project list --debug`**: This showed "No projects found" - this is strange since the config correctly specifies KAN. 

The `jira project list` command uses a different API endpoint than the issue search, so the project might not be accessible through that path even though issues are. The agent then ran `jira issue list` repeatedly with different flags - over 7 times - before accepting the backlog was empty, which was the most wasteful pattern. The file was correctly written to the workspace root where the workflow's hash check expects it.

The real improvements needed are straightforward: the agent needs to understand that "No result found for given query" with exit code 1 actually indicates an empty backlog, not a failure to retry around. And since curl isn't in the allowed tools, the prompt should explicitly tell the agent to stick with the jira CLI and never attempt curl fallbacks. The real problem here is that the agent kept retrying the `jira issue list` command multiple times when the first "No result found" response should have been the answer. The prompt needs to explicitly tell the agent that this message means the backlog is empty and to stop retrying with different JQL variants. There's also a permissions issue where `jira project list` returns nothing, suggesting the API token lacks visibility into all projects, though this doesn't prevent querying specific issues. The agent made nine identical `jira issue list` calls, all hitting the same "No result found" error without realizing this was the expected behavior for an empty backlog rather than a tool failure. I should add explicit guidance to the prompt that when `jira issue list` exits with code 1 and returns "No result found," that's a legitimate empty result—not an error to retry—so the agent can write the summary and stop instead of cycling through variations. This single clarification would reduce the run from ~15 tool calls down to just ~3. I'll place this note right after the JQL query explanation to make it clear that an empty result means the backlog is genuinely empty, stopping the retry loop before it starts.

content.text:
The key issue is clear: the agent ran `jira issue list` nine times with progressively looser JQL, then tried two `curl` REST API calls, all because it didn't know that "No result found for given query" with exit code 1 is jira-cli's normal output for an empty result set — not a failure to retry around.

The fix is a single sentence in the "Input scope" section of the prompt telling the agent what to do when it sees that message.

input.old_string:
```sh
jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved AND status != \"Development Ready\"" --raw
```

Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.
input.new_string:
```sh
jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved AND status != \"Development Ready\"" --raw
```

If this command exits non-zero with "No result found", the backlog is **genuinely empty** — write the "Nothing to report" summary and stop. Do not retry with looser JQL, run diagnostic commands, or fall back to direct REST API calls.

Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.



If this command exits non-zero with "No result found", the backlog is **genuinely empty** — write the "Nothing to report" summary and stop. Do not retry with looser JQL, run diagnostic commands, or fall back to direct REST API calls.

Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.


When the rubric fails because of missing or ambiguous information,
don't guess.

- Append (or refresh) a `## Needs human input` section in the
  description with one unchecked checkbox per open question:

  ```
  ## Needs human input
  - [ ] Should this use `/v2/users` or stay on `/v1`?
  - [ ] Rate limit — per-user or global?
  ```

- Humans answer in Jira comments. The agent never comments.
- Each run, before re-evaluating: scan comments. For each unchecked
  question that's been answered, incorporate the answer into the
  description and drop the question from the section. The section is
  rewritten wholesale — only currently-open questions remain.
- Any unchecked box → not Ready → *Awaiting human input* in Slack.

Only ask questions you genuinely can't resolve from the ticket text.

## Allowed mutations

1. **Edit description**: `jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input -b "<body>"`.
   Pass body as a plain quoted multi-line string. Don't use `$()`,
   backticks, or `printf` inside `-b` — they trip injection guards.
   If the body contains markdown headings (`#`), write it to a file
   first with `Write`, then pipe it:
   `cat .jira/desc-tmp.md | jira issue edit <KEY> --no-input`.
2. **Link blockers**: `jira issue link <BLOCKER> <BLOCKED> Blocks`.
3. **Create sub-tasks** under a parent (never standalone):
   `jira issue create -tSub-task -p <PARENT_KEY> -s "<summary>" -b "<desc>" --no-input`.

Do not comment on tickets — nobody reads them.

## Rubric (for descriptions and sub-tasks)

- **Title** *(sub-tasks only; never rename existing summaries)*: clear,
  concise, no type prefix.
- **Description**: what to do or what's broken; concrete file paths/
  symbols when they pin the work down.
- **Context**: why it matters — 1–2 sentences. Skip if self-evident.
- **Acceptance Criteria**: short list of testable conditions a reviewer
  can check.

## Forbidden

- Top-level issues. Every `jira issue create` must use `-tSub-task -p`.
- Transitioning/closing/reassigning, including to **Development Ready**.
- Touching anything outside the JQL.
- Bulk-editing — only act when the change clearly helps.
- Editing or commenting on tickets that already meet the bar.

## Slack summary (final step)

Write the run summary to `slack-summary.md`. Posted verbatim via
`chat.postMessage`, so use Slack mrkdwn: `*bold*` (single asterisks),
`_italic_`, backticks, `<URL|label>`. **Never** `**double-asterisk**`.

Build ticket links as `<{server}/browse/{KEY}|{KEY}>`. Read `server:`
from `.jira/config.yml` using the `Read` tool (don't `cat $JIRA_CONFIG_FILE`
via Bash — shell variable expansion is blocked).

### Format

First line is always the header. Then up to two grouped sections, each
introduced by a bold label and followed by a bullet per ticket. Skip
empty sections. Use the `DATE` value prepended to this prompt by the
workflow.

```
*Jira Ticket Agent* — <DATE>

*Transition to Dev Ready:*
• <link|KEY>: <one-line summary>

*Awaiting human input:*
• <link|KEY>: <N> open question(s) — reply in a comment
```

Every ticket touched must appear in exactly one section. If neither
section has entries, still write the header followed by `_Nothing to
report._` so downstream Slack posting still fires. Once written, stop
immediately — output no further text. Slack is the output.

lines: jira issue list -q "project = $JIRA_PROJECT_KEY AND resolution = Unresolved AND status != \"Development Ready\"" --raw

+If this command exits non-zero with "No result found", the backlog is **genuinely empty** — write the "Nothing to report" summary and stop. Do not retry with looser JQL, run diagnostic commands, or fall back to direct REST API calls.
Tickets in **Development Ready** are out of scope.

input.description: Create new branch for the prompt improvement

input.command:
git add agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Prompt fix: jira-ticket — treat empty backlog as done, not a retry target

When jira issue list returns "No result found" (exit code 1), the agent
retried 9 times with progressively looser JQL then fell back to blocked
curl REST API calls before concluding the backlog was empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create \
  --title "Prompt fix: jira-ticket — treat empty backlog as done, not a retry target" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
In run `.github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/2026-06-13/27461601553.json`, the agent ran `jira issue list` nine times with progressively looser JQL, then attempted two blocked `curl` REST API calls, before finally concluding the backlog was empty. The root cause: the agent didn't know that exit code 1 + "No result found" is jira-cli's normal output for an empty result set, not a failure to diagnose.

The fix adds one sentence to the "Input scope" section of the prompt explicitly naming this condition and instructing the agent to write the "Nothing to report" summary and stop rather than retrying or falling back to the REST API.
EOF
)" 2>&1

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/48

The run wasted ~12 tool calls because `jira issue list` exits with code 1 and prints "No result found" for both a real error *and* an empty result set — the agent had no way to distinguish them. The added sentence names the condition explicitly and tells the agent to stop rather than retry or fall back to curl.
